warnings.message: Some PDF pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some first body lines near the top of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some last body lines near the bottom of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some adjacent EPUB paragraphs look like possible faulty line or page breaks
Some list or roman markers appear embedded in prose instead of starting their own paragraph
Some paragraphs begin with large bare numbers that may be broken reference continuations
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Some bracketed/parenthesized/ordinal markers present in the PDF are missing from the EPUB
Some EPUB enumerators look like possible sequence jumps and need triage
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication

overlong_heading_candidates.text: II. That he is and is termed the Son of God solely on this account, and not upon the reasons mentioned by Mr B. and explained from his companions, is with equal clearness evinced.

III. ALTHOUGH the testimonies and arguments for the deity of Christ might be urged and handled to a better advantage, if liberty might be used to insist upon them in the method that seems most natural for the clearing and confirmation of this important truth,

I. THE death of Christ in this business is a PRICE, and that properly so called: 1 Corinthians 6:20, ' Ηγοράσθητε τιμῆς , — "Ye are bought with a price." And if we will know what that price was with which we are bought, the Holy Ghost informs us, 1 Peter 1:18,

II. It was a SACRIFICE; and what sacrifice it was shall be declared: — That Christ offered a sacrifice is abundantly evident from what was said before, in the consideration of the time and place when and wherein Christ was a high priest.
III. THE third consideration of the death of Christ was of it as it was penal, as therein he underwent punishment for us, or that punishment which for sin was due to us.
